content.claim: A CEU thesis reviewing Horn (2013) found that tracking schools show higher value-added than general schools but have deteriorating effects on test scores of students remaining in non-tracked schools, though this impact diminishes in the long run
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Finding based on single study, not replicated across multiple independent analyses
Long-run impact disappearance suggests short-term sorting effects may not persist
Causal claims from quasi-experimental design require strong assumptions about counterfactual comparability
Does not isolate extended-track (6/8-year) effects from standard tracking mechanisms
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
